posts.caption:
One of my favourite signatures of #SarahBurton, and something I’m excited to see her continue at #Givenchy is her extraordinary couture fringe technique. First fully realised for #AlexanderMcQueen Fall 2018, she drew inspiration from insects, butterflies, and metamorphosis to create the  butterfly-wing gown, dissolving into cascading silk threads.
These garments are so fascinating because of the level of craftsmanship required. The threads are dyed, blended, layered, and hand-applied to create subtle gradients of colour and movement. I had the chance to see one IRL and the delicacy of the work is unbelievable. The fringe requires constant brushing and arrangement, with even the smallest movement causing the threads to tangle, making the garments feel almost alive.
posts.alt_text: Photo by umar on May 13, 2026. May be an image of magazine, poster and text that says 'LNU No One Does Fringe Quite Like Sarah Burton'.

posts.caption:
Over 15 years since they first debuted, the @MaisonValentino Rockstud heels are back for Fall 2026 under the creative direction of #AlessandroMichele. Shot by Johnny Dufort and directed by Shayne Laverdière, the new campaign sees the newly updated rockstuds wandering through the streets of Rome.
First appearing in the #Valentino Fall 2010/11 runway collection, the Rockstud became one of the most recognisable shoes of the decade, becoming a defining visual code for the house. Now they return, reimagined with a new silhouette, cage design, and chiselled toe finished with a cap.
What do you think of the return of the rockstud?
posts.alt_text: Photo by umar on May 11, 2026. May be an image of sandals and text.

posts.caption:
In honour of #MiucciaPrada’s 77th birthday, let’s look back at one of the most beautiful examples of movie costume design - @bazluhrmann’s 2013 #TheGreatGatsby.
Working alongside @catherinemartindesigns , Miuccia and her team adapted 40 #Prada and #MiuMiu dresses from the archives for the film’s party scenes. Creating modern interpreation of the jazz age rather than historically accurate 1920s costumes. The garments were made with crystals, feathers, and fringe, chosen not only for their beauty but for the way they moved on screen.
Following the film’s release, an exhibition was held in New York and Tokyo to showcase the costumes designed for the movie. The dresses later reappeared in 2021 when @lorenzoposocco pulled several pieces from the archive for #DuaLipa “We’re Good” music video, including the infamous chandelier dress.
posts.alt_text: Photo by umar on May 10, 2026. May be an image of fur coat, dress and text that says 'Remember When Prada Dressed The Great Gatsby?'.

posts.caption:
Created in 2013, @songmont_official is redefining China’s luxury accessory landscape. The brand originated from the ‘Granny Team’, a group of artisans whose spirit and craftsmanship continue to shape the brand today.
Expanding its global presence, Songmont has collaborated with leading cultural figures including #RosamundPike, #KellyRutherford, #IsabelleHuppert, and #AlexaChung, alongside immersive exhibitions during Paris Fashion Week.
Their new Lattice Collection embraces the ancient leather-weaving technique Leng Bian. Inspired by the thousand-year-old murals of the Kizil Caves, the lattice is a perfect example of how #Songmont creates timeless, functional design through a distinctly Eastern lens. AD
posts.alt_text: Photo shared by umar on May 10, 2026 tagging @songmont_official. May be an image of purse, grocery bag and text that says 'This Chinese Brand is Redefining Luxury Bags'.

posts.caption:
It’s interesting to see more designers using plastic In exaggerated and inflated proportions and silhouettes, reminiscent of children’s toys - or what i like to call ‘polly pocket-ifiction’
#Chloé’s Spring 2026 jelly shoes are pure childhood beach holiday nostalgia, and their appeal lies in their deliberate wrongness - squeaky, translucent, slightly absurd? Another example i loved was #Loewe’s fall 26 moulded silicone coats and latex “lace” slip dresses, or  #Balenciaga’s 2021 3D-printed heels, based on a Barbie-style pump enlarged to surreal proportions.
The trend is so nostalgic, drawing from childhood toys (think #PollyPocket, Bratz, Barbie, inflatable furniture, and Y2K plastics.) Luxury is no longer defined by timelessness or practicality; instead, it is often intentionally unserious.
Images courtesy of Jeremyn Lee / Chloe / Loewe / romi.III / Marc Jacobs / Isidore Montag / Alessandro Viero / Gorunway.com

posts.caption:
Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) has been renamed to polyendocrine metabolic ovarian syndrome (PMOS), according to an announcement on Tuesday during the European Congress of Endocrinology.
According to the researchers, the term “polycystic ovary syndrome” frequently led patients and healthcare workers to associate the disorder primarily with ovarian cysts, even though many women diagnosed with the condition do not actually develop cysts.
The name polyendocrine metabolic ovarian syndrome is intended to better capture the condition's complex impact on the body.
Read more at www.shethepeople.tv

Out of a total of 1,008 dowry-related cases registered across the country’s metropolitan cities, roughly 87% were from the Karnataka-capital alone.
Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh, which is second on the list, reported only 48 cases.

Not every bridal detail is chosen to stand out loudly.
Some are noticed in quieter moments… through rich textures, soft drapes, regal accents, and fabrics that instantly make a bridal look feel deeper, warmer, and far more timeless. ✨
Velvet bridal details are becoming one of those elements slowly returning to modern weddings in the most beautiful way. Beyond trends that feel temporary or overly flashy… velvet brings a sense of old-world luxury that instantly elevates an entire bridal aesthetic.
And as 2026 bridal styling continues to embrace richer textures and more intentional details, brides are finding new ways to bring velvet back into their celebrations… from statement dupattas and blouses to embroidered accents, footwear, lehengas, shawls, and even wedding décor details that feel straight out of a royal moodboard.
There are bridal trends designed only for a season… and then there are textures that continue to feel regal no matter how many years pass.
Think of velvet bridal styling as more than just a winter-wedding trend… it’s about adding depth, richness, and a sense of timeless elegance that photographs beautifully in every frame. 💫
Because while fast-changing bridal trends may come and go… luxurious textures, heritage-inspired details, and timeless fabrics like velvet never truly lose their charm. ✨
Which velvet bridal detail are you completely obsessed with right now? 🤍

posts.caption:
Some weddings don’t rely on extravagant details to feel unforgettable… they do it through emotion, presence, and the kind of connection you can feel in even the stillest moments. ✨
There was something incredibly timeless about the way everything came together here. The richness of the bridal red, the softness of the ivory sherwani, the intricate embroidery woven into every detail… nothing felt excessive, yet every element carried so much depth and elegance. It was the kind of styling that doesn’t follow trends, because it already feels eternal.
And then there were the moments in between. 🤍
The way she looked at him with quiet admiration, the calmness in the way they stood beside each other, the comfort that naturally existed between them without needing too many gestures or perfectly posed smiles. Every frame felt less like a photograph and more like a reflection of something deeply genuine.
What made this beautiful wasn’t just how they looked together… it was how effortlessly their energy matched. Soft, graceful, and filled with the kind of love that never needs to prove itself loudly.
Some weddings are remembered for how grand they were.
And some are remembered because every glance, every touch, and every silent moment carried more meaning than words ever could. 💫

posts.caption:
Fashion styling is a job. Not a hobby. Not a passion project. Not something people do on the side because they love clothes.
It is a profession that takes years to build. The eye. The contacts. The knowledge of how the industry actually works across every sector, every brief, every type of client. That does not come from loving fashion. It comes from years of showing up, doing the work and figuring it out mostly alone.
And yet it is still one of the most undervalued roles in the creative industry.
Stylists are asked to work for free. For credit. For exposure. For a rate that does not come close to reflecting the skill, the hours or the experience involved. And somewhere along the way the industry decided that was normal. That stylists should be grateful for the opportunity. That loving what you do means you should not expect to be paid properly for it.
We are past that.
Behind every iconic image, every red carpet moment, every campaign that stopped you mid-scroll, there was a stylist. Building the looks, interpreting the brief, managing the pressure, absorbing the chaos so nobody else had to. Often without a credit. Sometimes without a proper rate.
That changes when stylists know their worth, charge it without apology and stop accepting the jobs that treat their career like a favour.
➡️➡️ If you are a working fashion stylist who is ready to do exactly that, comment STUDIO and we will send you the free trial link to The Studio of Stylist Elixir. Rate guides. Contracts. Portfolio tools. Masterclasses every Monday. Everything you need to build a career that reflects what you are actually worth.
Save this. Send it to a stylist who needs to hear it.
posts.alt_text: Photo by Stylist Elixir on March 14, 2026. May be a graphic of magazine and text that says 'Dear World, Fashion Styling Is a Job. IsaJob.NotaHobby. Not a Hobby. StyistElkir Stylist StylistElixir Elixir'.

posts.caption: If something needs such in-depth analysis to be understood than the assignment was a failure! It’s not a dissertation on the politics of 17th century France. It’s CLOTHING! It’s a fashion show and since fashion shows have been able to be seen by millions and consumed in photos and video for decades, those who are not in the loop to see the clothes for real are only left with what we see and to dare to say that just because a design has “References” or inner history negates it from being judged as what it simply LOOKS LIKE, is insultingly arrogant, something that takes 4,000 hours of the most intricate and elaborate technique… CAN STILL LOOK HORRID! All that time does not acquit it of its ugliness or stupidity of design! When all that history and back story sgd references WORK IN CONJUNCTION with the clothes to crest something magical (Blazy/Chanel) then the framework of all that in depth research and application of history is breathtaking, when it’s like JWA/Dior, it just comes across as over focus on ideas that then translate into nothing more than dissertations on a thought than the true purpose of what is happening… MAKING BEAUTIFUL CLOTHES! And for you to suggest that unless you’re erudite and informed of history that you shouldn’t comment is the height of condescension! Anyone can see! And no matter how layered the meaning, if it looks like all that time taken to produce garbage still looks like GARBAGE, it changes not its beauty because you have the knowing of the thought processes and HISTOIRE D’MAISON behind you! It’s pretentious and asinine
posts.alt_text: Photo by Stylist Elixir on January 27, 2026.

posts.caption:
You can be busy and stuck at the same time.
The week is full. The jobs are coming. The bigger jobs are not. The career feels busy on the diary and stuck on the rate, the portfolio, the kind of work you are getting booked for.
I have been there. Most working stylists have been there. The pricing, the pitching, the contracts, the rates — it is a lot to figure out on your own, and figuring it out alone is what keeps the career in the same place for years longer than it needs to be.
This is what mentorship is for.
✦ The Fashion Mentorship Program is six weeks of structured rebuild for working fashion stylists ready to move the career forward, together. ✦
What is inside:
💚 Weekly two-hour group calls
💚 Weekly thirty-minute 1:1s with our founder Emily, who has fifteen years in the industry working with celebrities, at GQ, and across commercial campaigns

💚 Six months of access to everything
What you leave with:

The program runs twice a year. August and February. Limited spaces. Payment plans available.

posts.caption:
A fashion stylist carries a lot more than the work the audience sees.
You know this. You have lived this. The visible work is the part that gets photographed. Everything else is the part that sits with you on the way home.
The pressure of a new job before it starts. The voice in your head that has to sound calm on the call. The brief that has not been read yet. The yes that has to come before the panic does.
The worry about a loan that has not come through. The PR who has not replied. The piece that has been promised twice. The plan B you are building in the background while plan A is still being chased.
The guilt of saying no to a friend. The favour that did not work for the rate. The friendship being held together carefully while you find the words.
The doubt before every big job. The private pride of a look that worked that nobody else will ever see.
✦ The job is more than the styling. The styling is the part that gets seen. ✦
Everything else is the part the audience never knows about. The part that runs in your head between jobs. The part that adds up over the years into a career that does not look like a career until you turn around and see it.
Anyone in this industry knows.
💚 If you are carrying more than the work you are showing
💚 If the weight of this job is heavier this week than usual
💚 If you need somebody to tell you that what you are feeling is normal
▸ Comment STYLIST to learn how we can help.
With love, Em x

posts.caption:
Illustrated journalism has always lived in the margins of serious news, despite doing some of its sharpest work.
In India, it has appeared as famine sketches, political cartoons and graphic testimony, often capturing what photographs or straight copy could not.
In 2026, that form received one of journalism’s most visible validations. Anand Radhakrishnan’s trAPPed, a Bloomberg graphic story on a digital arrest scam in India, won the Pulitzer Prize for Illustrated Reporting and Commentary, shared with journalists Suparna Sharma and Natalie Obiko Pearson.
The story follows Dr Ruchika Tandon, a Lucknow neurologist held under digital control for eight days by scammers using video calls, staged authority and sound effects. With few images to lean on, trAPPed turns fear, surveillance and psychological control into reportage.
In a media climate where India ranks 157 out of 180 on the World Press Freedom Index, the drawn frame feels less like a stylistic choice and more like a serious journalistic instrument.
Link in bio
By Arshia Dhar (@urseaa)
#illustratedjournalism #PulitzerPrize #visualreportage #Indianmedia #cultureandmedia
posts.alt_text: Photo by The Established on May 13, 2026. May be a graphic of magazine, poster and text that says 'Illustrated journalismi 1S not new. Anand Radhakrishnan's Pulitzer-winning trAPPed makes it harder to dismiss'.

posts.caption:
Absolut Creative Commune, Edition Four, gathers pace without fixing a single direction. Curated by SPRYK (@spryk.in), Absolut Creative Commune (@absolutborncolourless) brings together Abhijit Vinayak (@abhijitvinayak), Akshita Sinha (@akshitasnha), Amalendu Kaushik (@amalendu_k), Damini Gupta (@daminope), Era Namjoshi (@eranamjoshi), and Surabhi Banerjee (@surabhi_b6), each arriving with their own references, methods, and tensions.
Set against a moment where images are increasingly seamless and uniform, this edition leans into difference as a condition. The works don’t yield a single reading. They hold onto friction, authorship, and intent without smoothing themselves out.
The result stays open, shaped as much by process as by what finally comes into view.
#Collaboration #BornColourless #AbsolutCreativeCommune #AbsolutMixers

posts.caption:
Who decided jasmine was feminine and oud was for men? Fragrance has spent years pretending gender has a smell. This extends to packaging as well—pink bottle for her, black bottle for him.
But the new fragrance consumer is harder to sort. They are buying by note, mood, skin chemistry, memory and whatever self they are trying on that day.
Gender-neutral fragrance is not about smelling neutral. It is about removing the lazy labels from scent, and letting perfume do what it was always meant to do: sit on skin, shift with the wearer and say something more interesting than “for him” or “for her”.
Read more in the link in bio.
By Ria Bhatia (@riabbhatia)

posts.caption: Give it some time. My suggestion, stay away from outright hostile people, approach space wary people with understanding ( I will explain), and curious people with warmth. And do not group anyone based on their choices. That could be a common reaction, for example: Latest trending terms Radical Muslim, Hindu Mob, Sar tan se juda etc wtc. Secondly, please understand that it's not India specific and nothing will come out of trying to blame a common citizen or society or rituals. Once upon a time, even jeans were frowned upon, but slowly slowly without realising, jeans are considered as even for lack of a better word "prude". When I say understanding , I mean this... People judge. Not just queer, they would judge even a man with an unkempt look. If problem were Indian society then other parts of the world wouldn't have issues with LGBTQ. But they do. It's the fear of strangeness for most of them. More power to you, but I hope that instead of looking a society from hate pov, just chin up and ignore. Sooner or later, if your character is good, it will overcome and bias. As is the case with any person.

posts.caption:
If you love @oliviadeano’s concert style right now, you need to meet @cliopeppiatt. Her custom looks for The Art of Loving tour are basically a masterclass in modern stage dressing—heavily embellished, hyper-detailed, and covered in intricate beadwork that feels nostalgic without reading costume-y. There are clear references to icons like Cher and Tina Turner, but filtered through this softer, London-girl lens that still feels very current.
What’s amazing is how far the brand’s reach has stretched beyond the stage even before Olivia. Bella Hadid and Alexandra Saint Mleux have both embraced Peppiatt’s signature sparkle-heavy aesthetic, bringing it into completely different style worlds.
And yes, photographer and art director @szilvesztermako just shot the campaign for the brand’s new bridal collection, which honestly feels like a perfect match creatively.
Very dreamy. Very embellished. Very “main character leaving the party at 2AM.”
#ClioPeppiatt #OliviaDean #BellaHadid #SzilveszterMako #FashionDesign

The new Apple of Chloé’s eye is here! Apple Martin is officially fronting the house’s new high-summer campaign, Chloé à la Plage 2026, marking her first major collaboration with the brand. Directed by Chemena Kamali and photographed by David Sims, the campaign leans fully into that soft, dreamlike Chloé universe—sun-faded femininity, instinctive styling, and the kind of romantic summer imagery that feels half memory, half fantasy.
Everything feels light, effortless, and just a little surreal. A very strong first major fashion moment for her.
#Chloe #AppleMartin #ChemenaKamali #FashionCampaign #LuxuryFashion

posts.caption:
Working in fashion is often sold as a nonstop runway show, but the reality is that a lot of the industry runs on spreadsheets, deadlines, logistics, and an almost unbelievable level of consumption fatigue. It’s strange when your professional life revolves around identifying “the next big thing” while your personal brain is quietly begging for less noise.
Once you peel back the glamour, you realize most fashion jobs are actually about problem-solving. Managing chaos. Translating ideas into systems people can execute. The “unglamorous” parts—production timelines, budgets, analytics, inventory, endless inboxes—are usually where the real skill lives.
Anyone can love clothes. Not everyone can handle the machinery behind them. Happy Monday to the ones who do.
#FashionIndustry #FashionCareers #FashionWeek #TheCitizensPoste #MoodBoard
posts.alt_text: Photo by The Citizen’s Poste on May 11, 2026. May be a meme of magazine and text that says 'nobody told me working in fashion would involve this many spreadsheets posteposteposte.com'.

posts.caption:
The modeling industry really struck gold with Loli Bahia. Since her 2020 debut, she’s quietly become one of the defining faces of this generation—walking for Louis Vuitton, Chanel, Saint Laurent… some of the biggest players in fashion. Sharp features, insane versatility, and the kind of presence that makes a look feel instantly more editorial.
And the rise was fast. Born in 2002, Bahia started modeling at 17 and walked 65 shows in her first year alone after winning the Egeri Tour competition in Lyon while still in high school and pursuing music.
And it paid off. Face card truly never declines.
#LoliBahia #ModelingIndustry #Runway #FashionWeek #TheCitizensPoste

posts.caption:
Swipe to see all of the news you may have missed this week in fashion! The 2026 landscape is fully entering its “Fortress Brand” era. Instead of chasing viral moments, fashion houses are doubling down on heritage, technical mastery, and long-term cultural relevance. From archive-driven collaborations to legacy houses securing elite creative talent, the message is clear: stability is the new aspiration.
Brands are building worlds now, not just products. In a market flooded with trend cycles and AI imitation, provenance has become the real luxury.
Drop your thoughts in the comments.
#FashionNews #LuxuryFashion #Versace #RalphLauren #Adidas

posts.caption:
In case your weekend plans are loose and you need something to be insufferable about: Vintage fashion refers to clothing that is at least 20 years old, defined solely by its age. Archival fashion refers to specific, rare, and culturally significant items from a designer’s past collection—the grails, the runway moments, the pieces people spend years hunting down.
While all archival pieces may eventually become vintage, not all vintage items are considered archival.
The more you know. Happy weekending.
#VintageFashion #ArchivalFashion #FashionHistory #TheCitizensPoste #Moodboard

posts.caption: Warning to anyone wanting to buy with Vestiaire…. Don’t do it. Bought a LV purse but instantly questioned authenticity (literally as soon as I purchased it) went straight on chat about this. They kindly offered to verify it. The purse came to me and it definitely appeared fake to me…. No heat stamp inside, no serial number, very thin cheap dust bag. Hardware not typical of LV. Sent it off for verification. Waited a few days and today I get a notification to say it’s been verified and is authentic…. 🫢 I do not believe it for a second. Go on to chat to discuss, they say it’s got a serial number (seller also told me no serial number as well as when I looked myself) so I asked where the serial number is and could I have a picture… bearing in mind now they have it they are refusing to refund me and they are listing it to be resold. They’ve said they can’t send me a picture and can’t provide me where it is. Which all makes no sense to me whatsoever. I’ve reminded them that me asking for that serial number is me asking for that of my property. I don’t believe it’s real and therefore don’t wish to keep it but it is mine. Asked to speak to a manager and denied. I mean I may get a refund if it sells in 2yrs? What if it doesn’t sell at all? I imagine that I won’t see that listing and really they will do anything with it because they have it in their possession even though it’s mine?? Absolutely vile company. I am a small buyer I’ve not spent thousands. I know me not purchasing will make no difference really but I did want to warn others. I will never use them again, I’m going to make a claim on PayPal and leave a trust pilot review…. It’s a good job I guess that it wasn’t something worth hundreds or thousands. Very very bad company.

posts.caption:
Growing up in Chennai as the youngest of three siblings, acting wasn’t part of the plan for model student Samantha Ruth Prabhu (@samantharuthprabhuoffl). During her gap year after college, the then 21-year-old dabbled in modelling and ended up at a film audition for kicks. “I thought if I do get it, I’ll do it for a year and then go back to studying,” she says. “The idea was to do it temporarily, never make it a career. I’m sure my teachers were disappointed when I became an actor. I think some of them still are,” she laughs.
We’re not the first to ask her what her alternative career would be, but this time she has an answer: “I’m doing it now,” she says with excitement. “I think this entrepreneurial journey is what I’ve always wanted to do,” says the serial entrepreneur, whose work straddles films, beauty, wellness, fashion, food, and sport, all at once.
For our second anniversary issue, @chloechou13 flew to Hyderabad to chat with our cover star. Full interview at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
Samantha Ruth Prabhu’s (@samantharuthprabhuoffl) face card is immaculate. We notice this on a Friday morning, when we are on the set of our May cover shoot in Mumbai, and the star is getting ready for her first shot. At some point, the topic moves on to her upcoming film, and someone in the room asks, “Who’s the hero?” Samantha simply says, “I don’t do those kinds of films anymore.” We can’t help but grin.
It’s been a busy seven days for the star, between promotions and shooting patchwork for her upcoming film ‘Maa Inti Bangaaram’. “The story will be the hero in the movies that I do. The story is bigger than the producer, bigger than the actors in it.”
It isn’t about occupying the lead role on the top of the call sheet, she explains. In her career, Samantha has, in several of her biggest films, played characters orbiting male superstars, and knows all too well that female characters are often relegated to the role of a catalyst in the male protagonist’s journey, to propel his emotional arc as a narrative device. She, however, counterbalanced them with more textured roles, such as the journalist and storyteller Madhuravani in ‘Mahanati’ (2018), the emotionally complex Vaembu in the cult neo-noir ‘Super Deluxe’ (2019), and the 70-year-old trapped in a younger body as Swathi in ‘Oh! Baby’ (2019).
At the link in bio, @chloechou13 in conversation with the star.

posts.caption:
For our second anniversary issue, there was no looking beyond Samantha Ruth Prabhu (@samantharuthprabhuoffl). Two years ago, she almost quit acting when she was diagnosed with myositis, an autoimmune condition that causes muscle weakness, fatigue, and pain. It forced her to slow down and taught her a lesson at peak fame that she will never forget. “I was just so focused on running this race for which I don’t even know what the prize at the finish line was,” recalls the actor of the time when everything came to a grinding halt.
Since then, she’s renegotiated everything: her work ethic, priorities, even brand deals. “I realised all the checklists that I was so eager to tick off weren’t my own. They were checklists made for me by other people,” she says. Now at 39, she’s not only a top-billed actor but also an audacious producer, a serial entrepreneur, a fierce investor, and a chatty podcast host. At the link in bio, the multi-hyphenate talks to @chloechou13 about success, silent retreats, swimming, and what is likely to be her joyful next chapter.

The Quorum(@thequorumclub) began as an idea between Vivek(@vivek.narain) and Sonya Narain(@sonyajehan), to create a space where culture and community intersect. What started as a single members-only club in Gurugram, has since grown to Mumbai and Hyderabad, with Bengaluru coming up next. At its core, the idea remains the same – to offer a third space for like-minded, ambitious people who are culturally curious and seek out meaningful exchange in their daily lives.

“Some days are hectic, some are chill. Doesn’t really matter. If I’ve planned a face mask or a small beauty treatment during the workday, I just do it,” says Shanida, a Dubai-based customer success manager at Kendal AI, who recently went viral on Instagram for attending a Zoom meeting unapologetically with a face mask on. “Love that I went viral for the right thing,” she chuckles.
But it’s not just her. A number of younger workers are trying to fit in a salon visit during their lunchtime, which begs the question: can weaving beauty rituals into your 9-to-5 offer an escape from today’s hustle culture?

posts.caption:
This is not merely the story of Laila Tyabji stepping back from Dastkar. It is also the story of Indian crafts—and of a certain idea of India: plural, tactile, improvisational and deeply human—now confronting new fragilities. In a conversation with Shefalee Vasudev, Tyabji talks about embroidering with artisans, touching cloth, lives and hands, designing ambition for craftspeople and bridging everyone from bureaucrats to karigars. And why her idea of India may be harder to hold together today.
TVOF longform. For the full story, link in bio.
🖋️ @shefatwork
#thevofashion #lailatyabji #dastkar

posts.caption:
Hi, I’m Veronica 👋🏼 a sewing pattern designer based in Melbourne, Australia.
I create patterns, tools and resources that help you sew with understanding.
My work focuses on how fabrics behave, how garments are constructed, and how sewing connects us to the history of what we wear.
Sewing Patterns
My patterns are designed for versatility, wearability and fun. They include the Hazel, Freya, Odette, Audrey, Hera, Cordelia and the Sleeve Studio — each combining comfort with thoughtful design details. You’ll also find The Noelle, a free beginner-friendly pattern that introduces my approach to fit and construction.
The Paper Doll
A print-at-home paper doll designed to help you visualise and play with my sewing patterns. You can cut it out and colour it yourself, or use the pre-filled versions featuring my fabric prints and colour palettes. The set includes curvier model options and three skin tones to choose from.
The Conscious Consumer eBook
A practical guide to fabrics, sustainability and sewing with intention. It helps you understand fibre origins, weave structures and how to make long-lasting choices when you sew or buy clothes.
Digital Sewing Tools
Two paid calculators designed for precision:
• Shirring Calculator – works out fabric shrinkage and elastic thread length.
• GSM Calculator – estimates fabric weight and drape.
Available individually or as a discounted bundle.
My PDF sewing patterns are stocked digitally through @fabricgodmother , @spotlightstores , @thefoldline , @joans.finefabric and @thedrapersdaughter .
Fabric and Accessories
You can shop my fabric designs on @spoonflower and find my illustrated phone cases at @thedairy .
Everything I create is designed to help you sew with confidence, understanding and creativity.
For all enquires email us at hello@veronicatuckerthelabel.com

posts.caption:
To all the mums reaching for their dreams with their little ones in tow…this is for you.
Your kids are watching you, learning from you, seeing your passion and your commitment and your drive and that is EVERYTHING.
As women, we have been doing what we do with our little ones by our side for millennia. Whether that’s running the home, running the office or running the world.
Don’t underestimate how powerful and strong you are.
To all the women waiting for their little ones, I see you and I feel you. Your little ones will come, in time and sometimes in an unexpected way 🫶🏼
I know this can be a hard day depending on your situation, so no matter what, be the kindest mother to yourself 🫶🏼
And take the time to remember to look back to your matrilineal ancestors, what those women did and went through in their lives because that’s where our power comes from.
Happy Mothers Day from here in Australia 🫶🏼
posts.alt_text: Photo by Veronica Tucker the Label on May 09, 2026. May be an image of sewing machine and text.

posts.caption:
The most coveted colour in the ancient world came from a sea snail that smelled like garlic and cost more than gold.
250,000 snails to make one ounce of dye and workshops had to be built outside city limits because the smell was unbearable.
A woman spotted wearing the wrong shade at a recital was dragged from the room by Nero’s guards and stripped of her property.
What made it worth all of that wasn’t just the difficulty. Every other ancient dye degraded in sunlight but Tyrian purple deepened. Its permanence  was the actual luxury.
When Constantinople fell in 1453 the knowledge disappeared with the empire. The snails were saved from extinction almost by accident.
Part of The Goddess Project. I’ll also be doing a deep dive on this for my podcast The Culture of Cloth 🔗 link in bio.
Cover image: Rex Harrison as Julius Caesar in Cleopatra (1963).
Quotes from Kassia St Clair, The Secret Lives of Colour, used with her kind permission.

posts.caption:
A few days ago I posted about Mariano Fortuny and promised a full post on Henriette, because she deserved the spotlight rather than the margin.
This is that post.
Everything here traces to a single document…a patent filed in Paris on 10 June 1909, held at the Marciana Library in Venice. Mariano filed it under his name because he was present and the deadline required it. Then he wrote a note on the same document, in his own handwriting, naming Henriette Negrin as the inventor.
That note sat in the archive for 112 years but the V&A only updated their catalogue labels in 2021.
Mariano’s acknowledgement was there from the beginning…it just took the institution a century to follow it.

posts.caption:
“I don’t want to understand everyone; I want a world where I live and let others live. In New York City, I was staying in the middle of a huge building, surrounded by people of different races and religions, and there was no tension whatsoever. Sloterdijk even talks about how we need to learn a certain kind of discretion. That’s what bothers me — the idea that I have to understand your world. How am I supposed to understand your world when neither you understand it nor I understand my own? I’m very superficial in that regard. You have a neighbor — establish some kind of order, with basic respect, and don’t push yourself onto them, nor let them push themselves onto you. I take great pleasure in discretion. Maybe that comes with age.”
For the May Issue of Vogue Adria, we spoke with @SlavojZizeks, one of today’s most renowned philosophers, about the future of the left, the dangers facing modern society, one night stands, and revolution.

posts.caption:
A fashion magazine is rarely just a fashion magazine, as understanding cultural, political and social context has become one of the most important parts of publishing today. For the May Issue of Vogue Adria, we spoke with editors from Vogue editions around the world - @chiomannadi @britishvogue, @venabrykalin @vogue_ukraine, @_kerstinweng @voguegermany, @mrarnaut @voguearabia and @karlamartinezdesalas  @voguelatam - about the role and responsibilities of fashion magazines in today’s climate. In a world shaped by political tension, war, polarization and digital burnout, editors are constantly making choices about which voices to amplify, which stories deserve space, and how to balance fashion with cultural relevance and social responsibility. Read the full interview in May Issue of Vogue Adria.
Interview: @dazedsparkle

posts.caption:
We surveyed hundreds of Vogue and GQ readers about their wants from #AI in fashion. The key takeaway? Consumers are cautious, and the execution of early applications of AI in fashion is what’s holding them back right now. Those who have adopted its use for shopping are driven primarily by convenience and efficiency, but many are constrained by concerns around trust and authenticity. Tap the link in bio, where tech editor @AmyEOBrien and senior reporter @MalihaShoaib_ further unpack the survey’s findings — as part of our series the ‘Future of AI’.

Running is no longer just a sport — it’s an identity, with consumers drawn to brands that signal values, belonging, and function. This shift has opened the door for niche running labels to build cult followings at the intersection of lifestyle and sports technology. One such brand is New York-based @DistrictVision, which has become known for its high-fashion, high-tech positioning and research into the mind-body connection — placing it ahead of the wellness era.
Fresh from opening its first flagship in LA, founders Max Vallot and Tom Daly sat down with tech editor @AmyEOBrien to discuss what’s next. Tap the link in bio for more.

posts.caption:
For California-based Karishmakumari Jadeja and Manish Harnur, it was important that their wedding in Ahmedabad put sustainability first. The decor was mindful, with flowers used sparingly alongside layers of fabric and nada chadi, the ceremonial red-and-yellow thread. “I love flowers, so this was a hard one for me personally,” adds the bride. Her wardrobe followed an equally thoughtful approach. For the ceremony, she wore a Rajputi poshak layered with family heirlooms, including her mother’s wedding odhni. Her lehenga featured custom fish motifs in danka work, a 400-year-old metal embroidery technique commissioned specially by her mother. Read more about their wedding at the link in bio.
Image credits: @indianfootprints
#VogueWeddings

While Radhika Ambani’s wardrobe is rooted in a stellar lineup of beloved Indian designers, she’s also become a favourite muse for numerous international fashion houses. You’ll spot dreamy Erdem gowns, nostalgic Givenchy drapes, custom Chanel and the likes. Here’s a look back at some of her most impactful designer moments in recent times.
#VogueFashion
posts.alt_text: Photo shared by VOGUE India on May 13, 2026 tagging @erdem, and @swadesh_online. May be an image of one or more people, gown, dress and text.

posts.caption:
What happens when you put Meryl Streep and Anna Wintour—the two Mirandas, as it were—in a room together? A wide-ranging conversation about fashion, family, friendship, and yes, the forthcoming sequel to “The Devil Wears Prada.”
“In terms of Miranda, and coming back to that character 20 years later, I did think honestly about Anna, and tried to imagine what it was like to carry her responsibility and to be as interested in the world and curious as she must have to be,” Streep tells Greta Gerwig in Vogue’s May 2026 cover story. And what did Wintour do when she heard the rumours that “The Devil Wears Prada 2” was happening? She called Streep, of course. “I knew she would tell me if it was going to be all right,” Wintour says. “I trusted her implicitly.”
Read the full story at the link in bio.
Photographed by @annieleibovitz, Styled by @therealgracecoddington, Hair by @thebobbymichael and @dmchairmakeup, Makeup by @melissasilvermakeup and @dmchairmakeup, Tailoring by @billbulldresses, Produced by AL Studio, Production Design by @not.thestate. Vogue, May 2026.
posts.alt_text: Photo shared by Vogue on April 07, 2026 tagging @thebobbymichael, @annieleibovitz, @prada, @therealgracecoddington, @maryhoward_setdesign, @melissasilvermakeup, @billbulldresses, and @dmchairmakeup. May be an image of magazine and text that says 'VOGUE II SEEING EINGDOUBLE DOUBLE WHEN MIRANDA MET ΝΝΑ'.

posts.caption: Summer is upon us, and you know what that means: We’re dreaming of beach days. For some New Yorkers, a beach trip means heading out to the East End of Long Island for the weekend, slipping away into a world of bike rides, swimming pools, wineries, farm stands, and barbecues. One of the most alluring aspects of the area is what’s beyond those perfectly manicured hedges. From modern beach houses to Italianate mansions, head to the link in our bio for a look at exclusive photographs of Hamptons houses from a new book by Jennifer Ash Rudick.
posts.alt_text: Photo by Vogue on May 13, 2026. May be an image of magazine and text that says 'Inside Some of the Dreamiest Homes in the Hamptons'.

posts.caption: Alia! Ruth! Gillian! Hannah! From the star-studded premieres of “La Vie D’Une Femme” and “Teenage Sex and Death at Camp Miasma” to a mini “The Fast and the Furious” reunion celebrating the film’s 25th anniversary, head to the link in our bio to see all the best fashion from day two of the 79th annual #Cannes Film Festival.
posts.alt_text: Photo shared by Vogue on May 13, 2026 tagging @ysl, and @negga.ruth. May be an image of gown, dress and text.

posts.caption: Few pieces capture off-duty ’90s style quite like the raglan tee. Loved by everyone from Rachel Green to @ZoeIsabellaKravitz, the classic top still feels effortlessly cool decades later. Head to the link in bio for more on the trend’s staying power, and to shop Vogue’s edit of the best raglan tees on the market right now.
posts.alt_text: Photo by Vogue on May 13, 2026. May be an image of one or more people, blonde hair, sweater, turtleneck and text.

posts.caption: Ever wonder what the #Cannes Film Festival carpet looked like 20 years ago? To celebrate the start of this year's festivities, we're taking a look back at some of the most unforgettable looks from the 2006 carpet. Tap the link in our bio to see all the standouts, and to discover just how much the Cannes fashions have evolved over two decades.
posts.alt_text: Photo by Vogue on May 12, 2026. May be an image of magazine, carpet, dinner jacket and text that says 'A Look Back at the 2006 Cannes Film Festival'.

posts.caption:
When @LisaKudrow and Michael Patrick King pitched “The Comeback” to HBO in the early aughts, it seemed like a no-brainer. King was fresh off writing the two-part finale of “Sex and the City,” while Kudrow was wrapping up her 10-season run on a little show called “Friends.” The duo created Valerie Cherish, whose story has mirrored Hollywood’s changing landscape for 20 years, from reality TV to streaming and prestige dramas. For season three, King imagined Valerie starring in the first sitcom written by generative AI, “How’s That?”
With the season three finale now streaming on HBO Max, Kudrow sat down with Vogue's @keaton.kilde.bell to chat about 20 years of “The Comeback” and saying goodbye to Valerie Cherish—for good this time. Read more at the link in bio.
posts.alt_text: Photo by Vogue on May 12, 2026. May be an image of one or more people, makeup, people smiling, people standing, magazine and text that says 'Lisa Kudrow on Saying Goodbye to Valerie Cherish'.

posts.caption: In the nine years since its inception, the Loewe Craft Prize has become a beloved platform for preserving craft and creating community, and this year brought together 30 finalists from 19 countries and regions, including Haiti to Australia, chosen from over 5,100 submissions from 133 countries. Following extensive deliberation from the jury which included newly incumbent co-creative directors Jack McCollough and Lazaro Hernandez, this year’s prize went to experimental ceramic artist Jongjin Park for “Strata of Illusion.” Read more about this year’s Loewe Craft Prize winner at the link in bio.
posts.alt_text: Photo by Vogue Runway on May 13, 2026. May be an image of furniture, poster and text that says 'VOGUE RUNWAY THE LOEWE CRAFT PRIZE LANDS IN SINGAPORE'.

posts.caption: Fall may have only just begun in Australia, but that doesn’t mean the airy silhouettes being shown during this week’s resort collections feel out of place. The sun is still shining, and there’s a light breeze coming off Sydney’s famed harbor, which means we’re seeing the best that transitional dressing has to offer, courtesy of the city’s stylish Fashion Week attendees. See more of the best street style at the link in bio. Photographed by @thefourthcreative
posts.alt_text: Photo by Vogue Runway on May 12, 2026. May be an image of purse and text.

posts.caption: Opening on May 16, “Iris van Herpen: Sculpting the Senses” iterates on but does not exactly replicate the original 2023 show in Paris (organized by Cloé Pitiot and Louise Curtis of the Musée des Arts Décoratifs). Working in concert with the designer from New York are Matthew Yokobosky, senior curator of fashion and material culture at the Brooklyn Museum, and curatorial assistant Imani Williford, who have adapted the show—van Herpen’s first big splash stateside—to fit their environs. Yokobosky calls it a mid-career retrospective as this month marks @IrisvanHerpen’s 19th year in business. Learn more at the link in bio.
posts.alt_text: Photo by Vogue Runway on May 11, 2026. May be an image of text that says 'VOGUE RUNWAY WAY RUN IRIS VAN HERPEN GETS A RETROSPECTIVE IN BROOKLYN'.

posts.caption: It’s become a tradition at Vogue Runway to track looks that made it from the runways into the hallowed halls of the @MetMuseum and put on display in a Costume Institute exhibition. “Costume Art,” which considers the role of the dressed body in art and fashion, includes both forever favorites and emerging talent. Tap the link in bio for more.

posts.caption: Retail or hand-me-down, unwanted gifts from mom (or anyone you feel close to) take up physical and emotional space. In the latest installment of #Addressed, @liana_ava explores why letting go can feel so complicated, and how to move forward with less guilt and more clarity.

posts.caption: The artist who turned @KimKardashian into a living sculpture is currently showing in Paris. At the Sceners Gallery, far from the Metropolitan Museum, where Kardashian arrived at the #MetGala in a metallic orange breastplate with pert breasts and curvaceous hips, there are several examples of the art that inspired her. See more from Allen Jones’s exhibition at the link in our bio.
posts.alt_text: Photo by Vogue Runway on May 09, 2026. May be an image of text.

posts.caption: Spring always calls for a bit of a refresh, and sartorially speaking, a few wardrobe updates that feel intentional. Besides accessories, color and print are another great way to express your style, and the spring runways were filled with ideas that speak to an instinctive way of dressing. At the link in bio, we’re breaking down the strongest and boldest themes for you to shop and try.
posts.alt_text: Photo by Vogue Shopping on May 13, 2026. May be an image of magazine, sunglasses and text that says 'Bold ways to update your spring 2026 style'.

posts.caption: We are on the cusp of summer and, with any luck, the brink of a vacation or two. One of the questions @laurelpantin gets asked most this time of year is about packing, especially how to pack using just a carry-on. At the link in our bio, in the latest installment of #AskEarl, she shares her strategy for packing for a week—ok, or up to 10 days—in just one carry-on.
posts.alt_text: Photo by Vogue Shopping on May 13, 2026. May be an image of magazine, sunglasses and text that says 'Bold ways to update your spring 2026 style'.

posts.caption: There’s a way sitting down to journal seems to pause time, similar to how lighting a candle with a familiar scent can transport you to a specific place. For model and entrepreneur Rosie Huntington-Whiteley (@rosiehw), it’s precisely the slowness in these small rituals that became the foundation for @MémoireWorld, her new luxury keepsake brand that launches today, May 12. “I've always been drawn to objects that hold meaning and things that have been lived with, rather than simply owned,” Huntington-Whiteley tells Vogue. Shop the collection at the link in bio.
posts.alt_text: Photo by Vogue Shopping on May 12, 2026. May be an image of one or more people, makeup, magazine, newspaper and text that says 'Rosie Huntington- luntington-Whiteley' Whiteley's ist-launched brand is all about the keepsake'.

posts.caption: For summer 2026, @Moncler builds on its signature codes to make the case for warm-weather dressing that leaves room for spontaneity. ✨ Creator, Actor, PHENOMENON! ✨ On the June-July cover of Wedding Vows, we present the trailblazing @mostlysane, Prajakta Koli!
From building a YouTube empire to being the lead for Netflix Original Mismatched, from being part of Michelle Obama’s Day Time Emmy winning docu-series to being UNDP India's First Youth Climate Champion, her journey is nothing short of extraordinary.
With her debut romance novel set to launch this year, along with her first Amazon Prime Original Andhera, Prajakta’s journey is a testament to the power of dreams, hard work, and staying true to oneself. She continues to inspire millions with her authenticity, relatability, and unstoppable spirit.

posts.caption:
#wvFashion A nostalgic summer at Cannes ☀️ Alia Bhatt @aliaabhatt channeled a serene, vintage mood at Cannes 2026, pairing a  custom Tarun Tahiliani @taruntahiliani draped  ivory chanderi dhoti skirt, sculpted corset with a traditional gold nath, haathphool and Payal.
The look balanced Victorian structure and Indian heritage, elevated by a dainty parasol and intricate gold hathphools.
Moving away from maximalism, she embraced a sleek, rooted aesthetic that felt both refreshingly calm and globally relevant.
It was a quiet yet powerful fusion—where sharp couture met the fluid, old-world charm of a tiny bindi and heritage jewelry.

#wvFashion Thirteen years after actors Vidya Balan and Sonam Kapoor first brought the *nath* to the French Riviera, Alia Bhatt marks a graceful return for the traditional nose ring at Cannes.
While Vidya and Sonam paved the way with high-drama tradition and kundan stones, Alia’s latest look leans into a softer, vintage-inspired serenity that feels refreshingly modern.
Paired with an ivory Tarun Tahiliani corset saree, the gold jewelry anchors a fusion aesthetic that bridges Indian roots with global couture.

posts.caption:
@maudeapatow is ready now. Fresh off her return in the highly anticipated third season of #Euphoria, Apatow is stepping into a new chapter with the kind of clarity and control that only comes from growing up in real time—on-screen, off-screen, and alongside co-stars who have gone on to navigate intergalactic levels of fame and careers of their own. "Just thinking about us being little kids and having no idea what was in store to now… It's insane," she says.
In our May cover story, Apatow opens up about the roles that shaped her, the relationships that fulfilled her, the theater work that grounded her, and the rituals—travel, matcha, and all—that keep her moving forward. Read our full interview with the actress and director at the link in our bio.

posts.caption:
“I definitely relate to Hannah in so many ways. She is super quirky and fun, and she's nutty but owns it,” @ellabright__ tells us of her character Hannah Wells in Prime Video’s Off Campus. “She's very confident in the fact that that's just who she is, and I love that about her.”
Before there was #HeatedRivalry, there was Elle Kennedy's NYT best-selling romance series Off-Campus about a house of hockey-playing college roommates and the women who steal their hearts. At just 19, Bright is a newcomer in Hollywood, but is poised for a major breakout moment thanks to avid Off Campus fans and new audiences alike. We caught up with Bright ahead of the L.A. premiere to talk about the show's speed-dating audition process, her instant connection with Cameli, and even mastering piano and guitar for the role. Read our full interview at the link in our bio.

Major bag alert. Leave it to @haileybieber to introduce summer's richest-looking handbag color trend before the season even begins. Out in Malibu last weekend, Bieber was seen wearing a large bowler-style Chanel handbag (not yet released) in a color so chic we've been regretting all our navy, brown, and black handbag purchases ever since we saw it—ivory. While we wait for the It bag to be released, we found plenty of ivory handbags on the market that are available to shop right now. Head to the link in our bio to secure your bag and get the look for summer.

posts.caption:
The 2026 WWD Beauty CEO Summit, themed “The Innovation Imperative: Creating the Future of Commerce, Culture and Connectivity,” kicked off at @thebreakers hotel Monday evening.
At the event, top executives and industry experts addressed hundreds of attendees on a variety of pressing themes — from AI to the evolution of retail to brand-building in an uncertain macroenvironment and more.
Here, five key takeaways from the summit’s first full day on Tuesday. Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio

Photos: Marissa Alpe, Caroline Xia / WWD

posts.caption:
The United States Postal Service has tapped @ralphlauren to curate a set of commemorative stamps in celebration of America’s 250th anniversary.⁠
⁠
The selection by the U.S. Postal Service marks the first time an individual has been asked to curate a complete stamp issuance.⁠
⁠
Lauren handpicked 13 images from his personal archives that he believes represent the fabric of the nation. The images were made into stamps by the USPS and will be available for purchase beginning next month.⁠
⁠
Read more details at WWD.com — #linkinbio⁠
⁠
Report: @jeanpalmieri
posts.alt_text: Photo by WWD on May 12, 2026. May be an image of text that says 'WWD 2026 1776 6+2026 2026 USA FOREVER American Icons BUSINESS FEATURES USPS Taps Ralph Lauren to to Mark the U.S. 250th Anniversary With Commemorative Stamps'.

posts.caption:
Olivia Jade Giannulli is entering her founder era. The creator has launched @opiccola, a self-funded beauty brand debuting with the Bronze & Glow Balm — a dual-sided bronzer and highlighter stick inspired by her “less is more” approach to makeup.
After five years in development and trips to South Korea to perfect the formula, the first product drops Tuesday in three shades.
Read more details at the link in bio.
Report: @jamesmanso

posts.caption:
William Lauder followed in his late father Leonard Lauder’s footsteps on Monday night at @thebreakers in Palm Beach, taking the stage as the keynote speaker on opening night of the 2026 Beauty CEO Summit.
The evening celebrated the enduring legacy of the @esteelaudercompanies family, with industry leaders including @venuswilliams, @andreapreti88, @tommyhilfiger and @mrshilfiger gathering in support. Leonard Lauder — who died last June at 92 — delivered the landmark keynote at the summit’s inaugural event in 1997.
Read more from inside the WWD Beauty CEO Summit at the link in bio.
Report: @nikarajohns
Photos: Marissa Alper and Caroline Xia/WWD
